title: Prangos herderi
intro: Prangos herderi, also known as the Herder's Prangos, is a perennial plant species belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Prangos herderi include essential oils, flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Prangos herderi is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in combination with other herbs to create remedies for respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
taxonomy: Prangos herderi is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. This plant is native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. It is a member of the genus Prangos, which consists of several species of plants with similar characteristics. The exact classification of Prangos herderi may vary depending on the source, but it is generally accepted as a unique species within the Apiaceae family. Further research may be needed to determine its specific classification.

It is reported to be used in traditional medicine for treating conditions such as fever, headache, and digestive issues.
